goal: Enable safe production insertion for eligible normal scalar settings that currently use Hyprland defaults, without expanding unsafe write classes.
whatWasImplemented: Wired missing/default insertion into the existing safe-batch planner and executor for the narrow approved production scope.
Added insertion-specific plan records that show the target file, official setting, normalized config key, proposed value, exact inserted line, and review copy.

Restricted production insertion to reviewed single-file normal scalar configs with an existing normal root file that is not a symlink, generated, script-managed, duplicate, high-risk, display/render-risk, structured, profile/mode, runtime-only, or ambiguous.
Reused the safe-batch backup path, backup byte-equality proof, write path, reread verification, and restore-on-failure machinery for insertion changes.
Added deterministic safe-batch insertion section rendering and exact reread verification for inserted setting/value/line.
Updated live scenario harness coverage so the missing/default-only safe-env scenario proves the newly eligible single-root insertion path.
Kept unsafe missing/default layouts blocked, including managed, duplicate, multi-file/ambiguous, high-risk, structured, profile/mode, and runtime cases.
whatWasNotImplemented: No insertion into generated, script-managed, symlink/current-profile, profile/mode, multi-file, duplicate, high-risk, display/render-risk, structured, runtime-only, or missing target files was enabled.
No real user config insertion was attempted.
No runtime reload or mutating hyprctl action was added.
No production duplicate, high-risk, structured, profile/mode, runtime, or Hyprland 0.55.4 migration behavior was enabled.

safetyBoundaries.allowedTarget: eligible normal scalar missing/default setting in an explicit existing single-file root config

blockers: Production insertion remains intentionally limited to single-file normal scalar targets.
Source/include insertion, managed-file insertion, duplicate insertion, high-risk/display insertion, structured insertion, profile/mode insertion, and runtime mutation remain blocked.
nextRecommendedWork: Run full validation, then review whether a future architecture sprint should add explicit target selection for source/include insertion without weakening the current single-root gate.
